source: UC TAG Matrix PDF (June 2026) + individual campus pages

requirements: Complete at least 30 semester / 45 quarter UC-transferable units by end of summer before transfer
Complete at least one UC-E (English) and one UC-M (Math) by fall before transfer
Complete the 7-course general education pattern by spring before transfer
Complete 60 semester / 90 quarter UC-transferable units (junior standing) by end of spring before transfer
Complete at least 30 semester / 45 quarter UC-transferable units at one or more CCC; CCC must be last school attended
Require no more than 19 UC-transferable units in final spring term to reach junior standing
Must not exceed 80 semester / 120 quarter total UC-transferable units (after lower-division limits)
Earn no grade lower than C in fall/winter/spring terms after TAG submission
Submit TAG application via UC TAP between September 1-30
Submit UC application October 1-November 30; TAG major and UC application major must match
Submit Transfer Academic Update (TAU) by January 31
ineligibleStudents: Students who have already earned a bachelor's degree or higher
Students previously enrolled at a UC who intend to return to the same campus
Students previously enrolled at a UC who left not in good standing
Students concurrently enrolled in high school at time of TAG submission
sharedCriteria.note: Approximately two-thirds of UC transfer students do NOT have a TAG. TAG is optional but guarantees admission if requirements are met.

campuses.name: University of California, Davis
campuses.notes: UC Engineering requires 3.5 GPA. GPAs required by end of summer prior to transfer. Major-specific prep GPA requirements apply for selective majors.

campuses.name: University of California, Irvine
excludedMajors: All majors in the Donald Bren School of Information and Computer Sciences
campuses.notes: GPA required by end of fall term before transfer. Complete major prerequisites for specific majors. Art major requires portfolio.

campuses.name: University of California, Merced
campuses.igetcRequired: Required for Public Health only; recommended for all others
campuses.notes: Most accessible UC for TAG. All majors open. GPA required by end of summer prior to transfer.

campuses.name: University of California, Riverside
excludedMajors: All majors in Bourns College of Engineering (BCOE)
All majors in College of Natural & Agricultural Sciences (CNAS)
All majors in School of Business Administration
campuses.igetcNote: School of Business Administration highly recommends Cal-GETC/IGETC completion
campuses.notes: GPA required by end of summer prior to transfer. See UCR Majors Preparation Guide for major-specific requirements.

campuses.name: University of California, Santa Barbara
campuses.majorGuaranteeNote: L&S majors without a pre-major are fully guaranteed; L&S majors with a pre-major guarantee the pre-major status only
excludedMajors: All majors in the College of Creative Studies
All majors in the College of Engineering
campuses.notes: GPA required by end of fall term before transfer. Selective L&S majors (Bio, Chem, Econ, Math, Physics, Stats) require major prep GPA. Check ASSIST for details.

campuses.name: University of California, Santa Cruz
campuses.notes: All majors open for TAG. GPA required by end of summer prior to transfer. Additional major prep GPA requirements for some majors. TAG not available for Computer Science major (updated - now all majors open as of 2026).

campusPageText.UC Davis: Skip to main content UC Davis Open Search quick links Main navigation (extended config) ABOUT US Administration Inclusive Excellence Rankings Locations Sustainability Visit UC Davis UC Davis Health Student Safety ADMISSIONS Undergraduate Admissions Undocumented Applicants Graduate Admissions Professional Admissions Cost & Aid Apply to UC Davis ACADEMICS Majors Minors Graduate Programs Professional Programs Colleges and Schools Our Faculty Study Abroad RESEARCH From Labs to Lives Undergraduate Research Graduate Research Office of Research Library Museums and Collections Research Partners UC Davis Discoveries CAMPUS LIFE California’s College Town Housing Dining Things to Do Student Jobs Clubs and Communities Athletics NEWS Latest News In Focus Features University News UC Davis Magazine Social Media Student News Blogs Podcasts and Shows Transfer Admission Guarantee Breadcrumb uc davis admissions undergraduate admissions transfer admission transfer admission guarantee Guarantee your admission to UC Davis The UC Davis Transfer Admission Guarantee, known as TAG, is a written agreement that ensures your admission to UC Davis from any California Community College. Check the UC Davis TAG requirements to see if you’re eligible and what you need to start a TAG application. Review TAG requirements How to apply for TAG Custom Image Use the UC Transfer Admission Planner, known as TAP, to start your TAG application. Custom Image Review it with your community college counselor or UC Davis advisor. Custom Image Submit the TAG application by Sept. 30 via UC TAP. Important dates and deadlines Aug. 1 Start your UC undergraduate application on Aug. 1 for admission the following year. Sept. 1-30 Submit your TAG application via the UC TAP. Oct. 1-Nov. 30 Review your TAG decision mid-November. Submit your UC undergraduate application for admission and scholarships Oct. 1 to Nov. 30. Oct. 1-March 2 Apply for financial aid at studentaid.gov or dream.csac.ca.gov. More dates and deadlines TAG decisions Find out what the decisions on your TAG application mean. Learn about TAG decisions TAG FAQs Get answers to many of the Frequently Asked Questions about TAG. Review TAG FAQs Resources for TAG counselors Counselors, find the tools you need to work with students and TAG. campusPageText.UC Irvine: Jump to Header Jump to Main Content Jump to Footer Parents Counselors Current Students Search UCI Financial Aid and Scholarships Search Discover Study Afford Apply Request Info Transfer Students Guaranteed Admissions Related Majors Requirements Second Baccalaureate ADT Pilot Program TAG Roadmap Download our printable TAG roadmap for an easy, visual overview of the TAG process and important deadlines. TAG roadmap Apply Transfer Students Guaranteed Admissions Transfer Admission Guarantee Step up to what’s next in your education. UC Irvine's Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) program guarantees admission to highly qualified students from all California Community Colleges. Transfer students who meet eligibility requirements and complete the TAG application can secure guaranteed admission to any participating TAG major at UC Irvine. Majors Excluded from TAG For fall 2027 admission: Art; Business Administration; Dance; Music, B.Mus.; Nursing Science; ALL majors in the Donald Bren School of Information and Computer Sciences. For fall 2028 admission: Charlie Dunlop School of Biological Sciences: Biological Sciences, Biology/Education, Ecology and Evolutionary Biology Claire Trevor School of the Arts: Art; Dance; Music, B.Mus Donald Bren School of Information & Computer Sciences: All majors Henry Samueli School of Engineering: Aerospace Engineering, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering Paul Merage School of Business: Business Administration Sue And Bill Gross School of Nursing: Nursing Science School of Social Ecology: Psychology, B.A. School of Social Sciences: Business Economics, Cognitive Sciences, Economics, Psychology, B.S., Quantitative Economics Applying to TAG Create the UC Transfer Admission Planner (TAP) to start your TAG application Submit your TAG application between September 1 - 30 via UC TAP Submit the UC Application between October 1 - November 30 (Note: The primary major on your UC application must match the major on your TAG application) Submit the Transfer Academic Update via your UC Application January 31 Fulfill all remaining TAG requirements by the end of the spring term before fall enrollment TAG Requirements At the time you submit your UC Irvine TAG application: By the end of fall 2026: By the end of spring 2027: Students must meet all of the requirements in their entirety in order to be considered for TAG. Failing to meet and/or maintain any of the above guidelines automatically voids the guarantee. Situations That Affect TAG Eligibility or Approval Students who are concurrently enrolled in high school at the time of TAG submission Students who have already earned a bachelor, graduate and/or professional degree Students who previously enrolled at UC Irvine during a regular term (excluding summer) Students who are not in good standing at any UC campus Students who have not completed at least 30 UC transferable semester units (equivalent to 45 quarter units) at a California Community College Students who were not enrolled at a California Community College during their last term (excluding summer session) Students who require more than 19 UC transferable semester units (equivalent to 28.5 quarter units) during their last term in order to meet the minimum 60 UC transferable semester unit (90 quarter units) requirement Students who attended any college or university other than a California Community College and have more than 80 UC transferable semester units (equivalent to 120 quarter units) overall, after applying UC-lower division unit limitations and exclusions Students repairing a deficient grade in a required course (for UC eligibility or their major) if that course is still in progress at the time of application review Students previously flagged for falsification of their admissions application at any UC campus Students previously dismissed, suspended, or separated from any institution for student conduct violation The following scenarios will also nullify your TAG application: Incomplete or inaccurate academic history reported in the coursework section of the TAG application and/or UC Application Incomplete or no Transfer Academic Update submitted by the TAU deadline (January 31, 2027) No response submitted to our request for additional information by the stated deadline Submission of the UC application with a different primary major than your TAG application major Many students who are not eligible for TAG are still well-qualified for admission and are strongly encouraged to apply to UC Irvine through the regular application process without a TAG. For fall 2027 admission, the UC Application filing period is October 1 – November 30, 2026. Tips for a Successful TAG Application Review the majors you select closely. The major you select for TAG and your primary major on your UC application must match exactly in order for your TAG application to remain active. You will not be able to change your TAG major after you submit your TAG. You will not be able to chan

DISCOVER ADMISSIONS MAJORS AFFORDABILITY & VALUE COUNSELORS RESOURCES Transfer Continue Your Journey at UC Riverside Your hard work has put your goals within reach, and UCR can help bring them closer. Our award-winning faculty will mentor you. Our diverse campus community will embrace you. And our transfer-specific resources will help you along your path to a degree — and beyond. We’ve been waiting for a bold and brilliant Highlander just like you! Search by Topic REQUIREMENTS MAJOR PREP REQUIREMENTS TRANSFER ADMISSION GUARANTEE (TAG) VIEWBOOK APPLICATION INFORMATION ENGAGE WITH ADMISSIONS DATES AND DEADLINES STUDENT SUPPORT Requirements Transfer Requirements Complete at least 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable credit. Earn at least a 2.4 GPA in UC-transferable courses (2.8 if you're not a California resident). Some majors may require a higher GPA for admission selection. Complete the following 7-course pattern by the end of the spring term prior to fall enrollment at UC. Two transferable courses in English composition; One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: Arts and humanities Social and behavioral sciences Physical and biological sciences BACK TO TOP Major Prep Requirements Getting Into a Selective Major Most majors require you to complete specific prerequisite (major preparation) courses before you transfer. These courses help ensure you're ready for upper-division work in your chosen major. Be sure to take the recommended lower-division, general education, and breadth courses for your major. Some programs suggest completing the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) , a set of courses that California Community College students can take to fulfill first- and second-year general education requirements before transferring. However, other programs may prioritize major prerequisites over completing Cal-GETC. To find out what your intended major requires, use ASSIST.org, or contact a UCR admission counselor for guidance. For additional details, refer to the Assist Brochure. VIEW THE MAJORS PREPARATION GUIDE Marlan and Rosemary Bourns College of Engineering (BCOE) Requirements College of Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences (CHASS) Requirements College of Natural and Agricultural Sciences (CNAS) Requirements School of Business Requirements School of Education Requirements School of Public Policy (SPP) Requirements BACK TO TOP Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) Guarantee Your Admission to UCR The Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) offers guaranteed admission to California Community College (CCC) students whose last school is a CCC and meets the requirements below. 1 Create Your Transfer Admission Planner (TAP) – Use TAP, an online tool to track your progress and plan the courses you will need. Your TAP serves as your TAG application. 2 Meet TAG Requirements Have 30 UC-transferable semester (45 quarter) units completed by prior to TAG submission. Meet the minimum TAG GPA required for your intended major prior to TAG submission. Have at least one UC-M course completed by the end of fall 2025. Have the first UC-E requirement completed by the end of fall 2025, the second must be completed by the end of spring 2026. Meet major preparation requirements for all Selective Majors with no grade lower than a C. Have at least 60 semester (90 quarter) transferable units completed by the end of spring 2026 with no more than 19 units needed in spring term to meet the minimum 60 semester/90 quarter unit requirement. View the Majors Preparation Guide to check course requirements for your intended major. 3 Submit Your TAG Application by Sept. 30 – UCR offers fall admission for transfer students. Before you apply to UCR, log into your TAP to complete the UCR TAG application. 4 Apply Oct. 1-Nov. 30 – Submit your UC application. campusPageText.UC Santa Barbara: Skip to main content University of California, Santa Barbara MY STATUS MAILING LIST QUICK LINKS Main navigation Apply Events & Visits Majors Explore UCSB UCSB Communities Resources Home Apply Transfer Transfer Admission Guarantee Transfer Admission Guarantee The Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) offers guaranteed admission to California community college students who meet specific requirements. TAG is offered for the fall term only. To TAG, you must submit an online TAG application from September 1-30 and the UC Application for undergraduate admission from October 1 - November 30. TAG Overview TAG establishes criteria for guaranteed admission to UCSB. At UCSB, TAGs are available for all majors in the College of Letters and Science except performing arts majors that require an audition or portfolio review. Refer to the ASSIST website under articulation agreements by major between UCSB and your community college for more specific details on articulated courses. UCSB does not provide an early evaluation or early decision for TAG applicants. Applicants will be notified of their admission decisions in April. Fall 2027 TAG Requirements The following information outlines the UCSB Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) for the College of Letters and Science for the fall 2027 term only. MAJORS WITHOUT TAG: TAG does not apply to: Any major in the College of Creative Studies Any major in the College of Engineering Dance B.A., Dance B.F.A., Music B.M., Theater B.A. (Theater Design Emphasis), or Theater B.F.A. (Acting Emphasis) in the College of Letters & Science MAJOR PREPARATION COURSEWORK All transfer applicants are encouraged to complete as much major preparation as possible prior to transfer. Some majors require completion of specific major preparation coursework as part of TAG. For information about required major preparation coursework, refer to #11 of the Conditions of the Guarantee section below and to the articulation agreements by major between UCSB and your community college online at ASSIST. PRE-MAJORS Some UCSB majors require students to complete certain pre-major requirements at UCSB after transfer in order to advance to full major status. For majors with these pre-major requirements, TAG applies to admission to the pre-major. Meeting TAG requirements does not guarantee that a student will fulfill the pre-major requirements after transfer. Major preparation articulation agreements (found online at ASSIST) include detailed information about the pre-majors. After enrolling, students may contact UCSB academic department advisors to discuss pre-major requirements. UC-Transferable Units TAG does not apply to students approaching senior class standing (students with 80 or more UC-transferable semester units/120 or more quarter units) from accredited four-year universities or from accredited four-year universities and community colleges combined. All students should select their major carefully and complete as much major preparation as possible prior to transfer. A maximum of 70 UC-transferable semester units (105 quarter units) of lower-division coursework from any college or university will transfer to UC. Subject credit is awarded for courses taken beyond 70 semester units (105 quarter units). Any lower-division coursework completed at any UC campus, including UC Extension, will not apply to the 70 semester unit (105 quarter unit) limitation. All transferable UC units will apply toward the 80 semester unit (120 quarter unit) limit for TAG. All UC-transferable upper-division coursework completed at any four-year university will apply toward the 80 semester unit (120 quarter unit) limit for TAG. Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A Level exam units do not apply to the 80 semester unit (120 quarter unit) limit for TAG. UC allows a maximum of 14 semester/21 quarter units of Pass or Credit grades to reach the minimum number of units needed for admission eligibility. Please note that UCSB’s College of Letters & Science expects students to earn their degree without exceeding 200 quarter units. CONDITIONS OF THE GUARANTEE Submit a TAG application using the UC TAP tool during the filing period for fall 2027 admissions (September 1-30, 2026). Submit your UC Application to UCSB during the filing period for fall 2027 admissions (October 1-November 30, 2026) for the same major as the TAG application. Then, submit the Transfer Academic Update (TAU) by January 31, 2027 and respond to all UC requests for information. Complete at least 30 UC-transferable semester units (or 45 UC-transferable quarter units) by the end of summer 2026, prior to TAG submission. Complete the following required courses with C grades or better by the end of fall 2026: ▶ One English composition course (UC-E on the UC-Transferable Course Agreement) ▶ One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning (UC-M on the UC-Transferable Course Agreement) Complete a second English composition course (UC-E on th
campusPageText.UC Santa Cruz: Skip to main content Action Menu Begin Your Journey Image Deadlines Image SEARCH Image MENU Utility Menu MyUCSC People Calendars Maps A-Z Index Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) Transfer Admission Guarantee On This Page Get Guaranteed Admission to UCSC! UCSC TAG Step-by-Step TAG Decisions UCSC TAG Eligibility UCSC TAG Major Preparation Selection Criteria Get Guaranteed Admission to UCSC! A Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement ensuring fall admission in your desired proposed major, as long as you are transferring from a California community college and as long as you agree to certain conditions. UCSC TAG Step-by-Step Complete the UC Transfer Admission Planner (TAP). Submit your TAG application between September 1 and September 30 of the year before you plan to enroll. Submit the UC application between October 1 and November 30 of the year before you plan to enroll. For fall 2026 applicants only, we are offering a special extended deadline of December 1, 2025. Note: The major on your UC application must match the major on your TAG application. Submit your Transfer Academic Update (TAU) by January 31, 2026. TAG Decisions TAG decisions are normally released on November 15 of each year, ahead of the deadline for the regular UC application. If you have submitted a TAG, you can access your decision and information by logging into your UC Transfer Admission Planner (UC TAP) account on or after November 15. Counselors will also have direct access to their students' TAG decisions. Learn more about your TAG decision! UCSC TAG Eligibility The last school you attend prior to transfer must be a California community college (you may have attended colleges or universities outside of the California community college system, including institutions outside of the U.S. prior to your last term). At the time the TAG is submitted, you must have completed a minimum of 30 UC-transferable semester (45 quarter) units and earned an overall transferable UC GPA of 3.0. By the end of the fall term prior to transfer, you must: Complete the first course in English composition Complete the mathematics course requirement In addition, by the end of the spring term prior to fall transfer, you must: Complete all other courses from the seven-course pattern, required for admission as a junior transfer Complete the minimum of 60 UC-transferable semester (90 quarter) units for admission as a junior transfer Complete a minimum of 30 UC-transferable semester (45 quarter units) of coursework from one or more California community colleges Complete all required major preparation courses with the required minimum grades Have no more than 19 UC-transferable units (semester or quarter) in spring 2026 (or in the last regular term) to reach junior standing Be in good academic standing (not on academic probation or dismissal status) Earn no grades lower than C (2.0) in UC-transferable coursework for the fall, winter, and/or spring terms after TAG submission and prior to transfer. The following students are NOT eligible for UCSC TAG: Students in or approaching senior standing: more than 80 semester (120 quarter) units of combined lower-and upper-division coursework, after applying UC transfer credit limitations. If you only attended a California Community College, you will not be considered in or approaching senior standing. Former UC students who are not in good standing at the UC campus they attended (less than a 2.0 GPA at UC) Former UCSC students, who must apply for readmission to the campus Students who have earned a bachelor’s degree or higher Students who are currently enrolled at a high school UCSC TAG Major Preparation Selection Criteria For all majors except those listed below, TAG is based on the criteria above only. Please see our Non-Screening Majors page for more information about these majors. For the majors listed below, in addition to the criteria above, additional major selection criteria apply. To access these criteria, please click on the link for each major, which will take you to the screening criteria in the General Catalog. You must complete your major preparation coursework and satisfy any major selection criteria by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. Agroecology Applied Mathematics Applied Physics Art and Design: Games and Playable Media Biochemistry and Molecular Biology Biology, BA Biology, BS Biomolecular Engineering and Bioinformatics Biotechnology Business Management Economics Chemistry, BA Chemistry, BS Cognitive Science Computer Engineering Computer Science, BA Computer Science, BS Computer Science: Computer Game Design Earth Sciences Ecology and Evolution Economics Economics/Mathematics Combined Major Electrical Engineering Environmental Sciences Environmental Studies Environmental Studies/Biology Combined Major Environmental Studies/Economics Combined Major Global and Community Health, B.S. Global Economics Marine Biology Mathematics, BA Mathematics, BS Mathematics Education Mathematics Theory

rawPdfText:
UC Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) for students applying for fall 2027 or spring 2028 admission
Six University of California (UC) campuses offer guaranteed admission to students from all California community colleges (CCC). Interested students
must meet both the shared TAG criteria as well as campus-specific requirements to qualify for a TAG. By participating in a TAG program, students can ensure
their admission to a specific UC campus. Also, students interested in pursuing the Pathways+ transfer option will use a TAG to secure their guarantee.
Shared TAG criteria
To qualify for a fall 2027 TAG at any one of the six participating campuses, students must meet the following criteria:
1. Must have completed at least 30 semester/45 quarter UC-transferable units at the time of TAG submission (end of summer 2026);
2. Must complete at least one UC-E (English) and one UC-M (Math) by fall 2026;
3. Must complete the remaining 7-course pattern by spring 2026;
4. Must complete 60 semester/90 quarter UC-transferable units (junior standing) by the end of spring 2027;
5. Must complete at least 30 semester/45 quarter UC-transferable units at one or more CCC by spring 2027, and a CCC must be the last school of
attendance in a regular session;
6. Must require no more than 19 UC-transferable units (semester or quarter) in spring 2027 (or in the last regular term) to reach junior standing;
7. Must not exceed 80 semester/120 quarter total UC-transferable units, after applying lower-division limits (students with only CCC coursework and
AP/IB/A level credits will not exceed this limit);
8. Must earn no grade lower than C in the current fall, winter and/or spring terms and remain in good academic standing;
9. Must submit a TAG application through the UC Transfer Admission Planner (UC TAP) between September 1 and September 30, 2026;
10. Must submit the UC application between October 1 and November 30, 2026, and the TAG major and application major for the TAG campus must match;
11. Must submit a Transfer Academic Update (TAU) by January 31, 2027; and,
12. Must meet all campus-specific TAG criteria outlined in the chart below.
Not eligible for TAG
The following students are not eligible for a TAG at any campus:
• Students who have already earned a bachelor’s or higher degree;
• Students previously enrolled at a UC campus who intend to return to the same UC campus;
• Students previously enrolled at a UC campus who were not in good standing when they left that UC; and,
• Students concurrently enrolled in high school at the time of TAG submission.
Please note: Transfer students do not have to participate in TAG to be considered for admission. In fact, approximately two-thirds of transfer students
admitted to UC do not have a TAG. Non-TAG students simply apply for UC admission during the appropriate application filing period. Transfer students are
responsible for accurately reporting all college coursework on their TAG and UC applications.
Specific information about TAG at each of the six participating UC campuses is provided in the table below.

• Second UC-E must be completed by end of summer 2027.
• UC-M must be completed by end of fall 2027.
• All other TAG qualifications must be met by end of fall 2027.

Graduate, Undergraduate and Equity Affairs – Undergraduate Admissions
2027–28 TAG Matrix summary of changes
Shared criteria:
• Shared requirement #8 has been revised to state: “Students must earn no grade lower than C in the current fall, winter and/or
spring terms and remain in good academic standing”
Campus updates:
• UC Merced: The final page of the TAG Matrix includes guidance for spring 2028 TAG applicants.
• UC Riverside: Several academic school names have been updated to reflect current institutional nomenclature.
• UC Davis: The Business major is not available through TAG.
• UC Santa Barbara:
• The Statistics and Applied Probability major requires completion of major preparation coursework and a specified major
preparation GPA.
• The Theater B.A. (Theater Design emphasis) major is no longer available through TAG.
• UC Santa Cruz: TAG is currently available for all majors.
• UC Irvine: In addition to the current majors excluded from TAG, applicants applying for fall 2028 admission will no longer be
able to utilize TAG for the following majors: Aerospace Engineering, Biological Sciences, Biology/Education, Business
Economics, Cognitive Sciences, Computer Engineering, Ecology & Evolutionary Biology, Economics, Electrical Engineering,
Mechanical Engineering, Psychology (B.A. and B.S); and Quantitative Economics.
Accurate as of June 9, 2026, for the fall 2027 and spring 2028 TAG application cycle. Subject to change. For future TAG application cycle information refer to each individual UC campus admissions
website.
